title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source tool with over 180,000 GitHub stars. When run with MiniMax's m3 model, it offers significant cost reductions in inference pricing.
writeup: This video demonstrates Minimax M3, a model the author argues is the first to sit above the price-to-capability curve, outperforming Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 on SWE Pro, SVG BenchBench, and BrowseComp benchmarks at roughly 10 to 20 times lower cost during its current 50% off promotion. The core mechanism is Minimax Sparse Attention (MSA), which reads only contextually relevant tokens rather than the full context, yielding a 1-million-token window at an estimated 1/20th of standard transformer compute. The video walks through connecting Minimax M3 as the backend for two open-source agents: Hermes Agent (a terminal-based autonomous agent) and Open Code (a Claude Code alternative), running both simultaneously on the same subscription plan. A live cost check mid-demo shows 1 cent spent and 1% of weekly quota consumed after 60,000 tokens across both agents, making always-on agent loops economically viable.
key_points: Minimax M3 is claimed to be the first model above the price-to-capability line, benchmarking above Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 on SWE Pro and SVG BenchBench while costing $0.60 per million input tokens versus Opus at $5.00, a roughly 8x base difference that widens to 10-20x with the current 50% discount.

MSA (Minimax Sparse Attention) is the architectural basis: instead of attending to every token in context on each forward pass, it identifies and reads only the relevant portions, enabling 1M-token context at an estimated 1/20th of standard compute.

The $20/month Minimax subscription plan provides 1.7 billion tokens per month; the $50/month plan provides 5.1 billion, making it feasible to run long-horizon autonomous agent loops such as Hermes Agent's slash-goal feature, which can sustain over 24 hours and up to 2,000 tool calls without human intervention.

Hermes Agent and Open Code both accept Minimax M3 via a subscription key from the Minimax console, with no per-token API billing required: Hermes is configured at install time by selecting the Minimax provider and pasting the key, and Open Code connects via the /connect provider command.

A live deep-research task (cataloguing AI, tech, and business events across Katowice, Krakow, and Warsaw over 90 days into a Markdown file) ran concurrently with an SVG animation generation task, consuming 1 cent and 1% of the weekly token quota after processing roughly 60,000 tokens across both sessions.
